is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of Plasmodium falciparum drug resistance (DHPS/DHFR mutations).

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports DHFR codons 51,59,108 and DHPS 436,437,540,581,613 in Tables 1-3.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Reports genotypes of individual laboratory clones/lines (HB3, Dd2, 3D7, K1, FCR3, M24, etc.) and cross progeny, not population frequencies with numerator/denominator counts.

sub_country_location.evidence: Lines listed only by broad origin (e.g. 'Kenya', 'Gambia', 'Africa', 'Honduras', 'Thailand', 'Indochina'); these are lab strains, not field surveys with sub-country sites.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: No collection dates given for the parasite lines used; these are established laboratory reference strains.

african_field_samples.evidence: All parasites are laboratory-adapted clones/lines and cross progeny cultured in vitro (HB3-Dd2 genetic cross, 3D7, FCR3, etc.), not field/clinical isolates freshly collected in Africa.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical laboratory study with in vitro drug assays and DNA sequencing.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Uses well-established laboratory lines (HB3, Dd2, 3D7, FCR3, K1, M24) and the HB3-Dd2 genetic cross described previously (Wellems et al., 1990; Walker-Jonah et al., 1992); genotypes previously reported (Brooks et al., 1994).

supplement.evidence: No supplementary frequency data referenced; the paper is a lab-strain characterization, not a field survey.

assessment.summary: Laboratory study characterizing SDX resistance in P. falciparum reference clones and HB3-Dd2 cross progeny via in vitro assays and DHPS/DHFR sequencing. It reports genotypes of individual lab strains, not population frequencies from African field isolates, and provides no collection dates or sub-national field locations.
exclusion_reasons: Uses laboratory-adapted strains and cross progeny, not African field/clinical isolates
No sub-country field locations; only broad strain origins
No collection dates/time windows for the parasites
